title: Keratitis documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Microbial keratitis (bacterial, fungal)
source.population: People with suspected bacterial or fungal microbial keratitis.
source.setting: Primary, first-contact, and specialist eye care.
source.exclusions: Exact for sight-threatening keratitis features and emergency referral context; UAE pathways and treatment require clinician review.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.

assessment.text: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

assessment.text: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.
